author: @eXeYeZe-404
text: Would putting on the vents for my furnace provide enough flutter to be worth covering all the vents in my place?

replies.author: @mookiemorjax
replies.text:
Very probably no. A furnace fan uses a tiny slice of a home's total power usage, and in a best case scenario, you'd only harvest a tiny fraction of the vent output (if you harvested 100% of the wind energy coming out of the vents, nothing would come out of the vent). 

Worst case scenario, you make the fan work harder, cancelling out your efforts and shortening the fan lifespan. 

Seems like the juice isn't nearly worth the squeeze on this one.

replies.author: @eXeYeZe-404
replies.text: @mookiemorjax  thanks. I was thinking would it be powerful to charge a night light without interfering with the heat or air-conditioning.   Nothing major just tinkering and thinking.
